tasks.title: Xcode Project Setup & Core Data Schema
tasks.description: Create the Xcode project with SwiftUI lifecycle, configure Core Data stack, and define the entity schema for WorkoutTemplate, Exercise, WorkoutSession, and SetRecord.
tasks.details:
1. Create a new Xcode project named 'GymBro' with SwiftUI App lifecycle.
2. Enable Core Data in the project.
3. Define Core Data entities:
   - WorkoutTemplate: id (UUID), name (String), createdAt (Date), exercises (relationship to Exercise, ordered, cascade delete)
   - Exercise: id (UUID), name (String), sets (Int16), reps (Int16), restSeconds (Int16), order (Int16), template (relationship to WorkoutTemplate)
   - WorkoutSession: id (UUID), date (Date), duration (Int32), completed (Bool), templateName (String), template (optional relationship to WorkoutTemplate)
   - SetRecord: id (UUID), weight (Double), repsDone (Int16), fatigue (Int16), setIndex (Int16), exerciseName (String), session (relationship to WorkoutSession)
4. Create PersistenceController with shared instance and preview support.
5. Set up MVVM folder structure: Models/, ViewModels/, Views/, Services/, Utilities/.
6. Configure Info.plist for HealthKit and microphone usage descriptions.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify project builds and runs on simulator. Verify Core Data stack initializes without errors. Create and fetch a test entity to validate schema.

tasks.title: Core Data Models & Repository Layer
tasks.description: Implement Swift model structs, Core Data CRUD operations, and a repository pattern for clean data access across the app.
tasks.details:
1. Create Swift structs mirroring Core Data entities for use in ViewModels (WorkoutTemplateModel, ExerciseModel, WorkoutSessionModel, SetRecordModel).
2. Implement WorkoutRepository with methods:
   - createTemplate(name:exercises:) -> WorkoutTemplateModel
   - fetchAllTemplates() -> [WorkoutTemplateModel]
   - updateTemplate(id:name:exercises:)
   - deleteTemplate(id:)
3. Implement SessionRepository with methods:
   - createSession(templateId:) -> WorkoutSessionModel
   - completeSession(id:duration:)
   - fetchAllSessions() -> [WorkoutSessionModel]
   - fetchSessions(from:to:) -> [WorkoutSessionModel]
   - addSetRecord(sessionId:exerciseName:weight:reps:fatigue:setIndex:)
4. Add proper error handling with custom WorkoutError enum.
5. Use @FetchRequest wrappers where appropriate in views.
tasks.testStrategy: Write unit tests for CRUD operations. Test creating a template with exercises, fetching it back, updating, and deleting. Verify cascade delete removes exercises when template is deleted.

tasks.title: Timer Engine with State Machine
tasks.description: Build a precise timer engine using DispatchSourceTimer with a state machine managing workout flow: IDLE → EXERCISE_RUNNING → REST_RUNNING → NEXT_SET → NEXT_EXERCISE → COMPLETED.
tasks.details:
1. Create TimerEngine class as ObservableObject.
2. Define WorkoutState enum: idle, exerciseRunning, restRunning, nextSet, nextExercise, completed.
3. Implement DispatchSourceTimer for precise 1-second ticks:
   - timer = DispatchSource.makeTimerSource()
   - schedule(deadline: .now(), repeating: 1)
   - Update on main queue
4. Published properties: currentState, remainingSeconds, totalElapsedSeconds, currentExerciseIndex, currentSetIndex.
5. Methods: start(), pause(), resume(), skip(), goBack(), reset().
6. State transition logic:
   - IDLE → start() → EXERCISE_RUNNING
   - EXERCISE_RUNNING → timer expires → REST_RUNNING (haptic brief)
   - REST_RUNNING → timer expires → NEXT_SET or NEXT_EXERCISE (haptic double)
   - Last set of last exercise → COMPLETED
7. Haptic feedback via UIImpactFeedbackGenerator:
   - .light for exercise end
   - .medium + .medium (double tap) for rest end
8. Keep screen awake: UIApplication.shared.isIdleTimerDisabled = true during workout.
9. Track total elapsed time independently of set/rest timers.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test state transitions. Verify timer counts down correctly. Test pause/resume doesn't lose time. Test skip advances to correct next state. Verify haptic triggers at correct state transitions.

tasks.title: Workout Template Creation & Editing UI
tasks.description: Build the screen for creating and editing workout templates with exercise list, drag-to-reorder, and quick templates.
tasks.details:
1. Create TemplateListView showing all saved templates.
2. Create TemplateEditorView with:
   - TextField for template name
   - Picker for split type (Push, Pull, Legs, Upper, Lower, Full Body, Custom)
   - List of exercises with drag-to-reorder (.onMove modifier)
   - Each exercise row: name, sets x reps, rest time
   - Swipe to delete exercise
   - '+ Add Exercise' button
3. Create ExerciseEditorSheet:
   - Exercise name (with search/autocomplete from common exercises)
   - Stepper for sets (1-10)
   - Stepper for reps (1-50)
   - Stepper for rest seconds (15-300, step 15)
4. Create TemplateEditorViewModel handling CRUD via WorkoutRepository.
5. Support template duplication (swipe action on template list).
6. Provide 3-4 quick templates (PPL, Upper/Lower, Full Body) that can be loaded.
7. Follow dark mode design: black background, high contrast text, CTAs at bottom.
tasks.testStrategy: Test creating a template with multiple exercises. Verify reordering persists. Test editing an existing template. Test duplicate creates independent copy. Verify quick templates load correctly.

tasks.title: Today's Workout Screen (1-Tap Start)
tasks.description: Build the home/landing screen showing today's scheduled workout with a prominent single-tap start CTA.
tasks.details:
1. Create TodayView as the app's main/landing tab.
2. Layout per wireframe:
   - Header: 'Oggi - [Template Name]' (24pt font)
   - Estimated time: calculated from total sets * (exercise_time + rest_time) (18pt font)
   - Exercise summary list: exercise name + sets x reps
   - Large CTA button at bottom: 'INIZIA ALLENAMENTO' (70pt height, dark blue)
3. Create TodayViewModel:
   - Determine today's template (simple rotation or user selection)
   - Calculate estimated duration
   - Handle start action → navigate to ActiveWorkoutView
4. Haptic feedback on start tap (.light impact)
5. If no template assigned, show prompt to create or select one.
6. Dark mode: black background (#000000), CTA dark blue (#1a3a5c).
7. Support navigation to template list for changing today's workout.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify correct template shows for today. Test estimated time calculation accuracy. Verify 1-tap start navigates to active workout. Test empty state when no templates exist.

tasks.title: Active Workout Session Screen
tasks.description: Build the main workout execution screen showing current exercise, timer, weight controls, and set progress with zero-scroll layout.
tasks.details:
1. Create ActiveWorkoutView with zero-scroll layout:
   - Exercise name (top, large font)
   - Set progress: 'Serie [current]/[total]' 
   - Timer display: large countdown (72pt font, monospaced)
   - Current weight display with +/- buttons (step: 2.5kg)
   - Reps done counter with +/- buttons
   - Auto-advance indicator
2. Create ActiveWorkoutViewModel:
   - Initialize with template exercises
   - Connect to TimerEngine
   - Track per-set: weight, reps done
   - Save SetRecord on set completion
   - Create WorkoutSession on start, complete on finish
3. Bottom controls: Pause, Skip, End Workout.
4. Auto-advance: when rest timer ends, automatically move to next set/exercise.
5. Display state-appropriate content:
   - EXERCISE_RUNNING: show exercise timer + weight/reps input
   - REST_RUNNING: show rest countdown + 'Prossimo: [next exercise/set]'
   - COMPLETED: show summary and save
6. Disable idle timer during workout.
7. Weight defaults to last used weight for same exercise.
tasks.testStrategy: Test full workout flow from start to completion. Verify set records are saved correctly. Test weight/reps adjustment. Verify auto-advance works between sets and exercises. Test pause and resume.

tasks.title: Set Logging & Weight/Reps Input
tasks.description: Implement reliable per-set logging of weight, reps done, and optional fatigue rating with persistent storage.
tasks.details:
1. Enhance SetRecord saving in ActiveWorkoutViewModel:
   - On each set completion, save: weight, repsDone, fatigue (optional 1-10), setIndex, exerciseName
   - Associate with current WorkoutSession
2. Weight input component:
   - Large +/- buttons (44x44pt minimum touch target)
   - Step: 2.5kg (configurable)
   - Long press for fast increment
   - Display in kg (future: lb toggle)
3. Reps input component:
   - +/- buttons
   - Default to template reps value
   - Highlight if different from target
4. Optional fatigue slider (1-10) shown after completing each exercise (not each set).
5. Pre-populate weight from last session's same exercise.
6. Save immediately on completion (no batch save at end).
7. Handle edge cases: 0 weight (bodyweight), partial sets.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify set records persist to Core Data. Test weight pre-population from previous sessions. Test edge cases: bodyweight exercises, partial sets. Verify data integrity after app restart.

tasks.title: Auto-Advance Between Exercises & Sets
tasks.description: Implement automatic progression through sets and exercises with smooth transitions and clear visual/haptic feedback.
tasks.details:
1. In TimerEngine, implement auto-advance logic:
   - After EXERCISE_RUNNING completes → auto-transition to REST_RUNNING
   - After REST_RUNNING completes → auto-transition to NEXT_SET or NEXT_EXERCISE
   - Brief 3-second countdown overlay before advancing
2. Visual transitions:
   - Animated state change between exercise and rest
   - Preview of next exercise during rest period
   - Set counter updates smoothly
3. Haptic pattern:
   - Single vibration → exercise time done, rest starts
   - Double vibration → rest done, next set/exercise starts
4. Override: user can tap to advance early or delay.
5. Handle last set of last exercise → transition to COMPLETED state.
6. Sound option (optional, off by default): subtle chime on transitions.
7. Ensure smooth animation between states (withAnimation).
tasks.testStrategy: Test complete auto-advance flow through multi-exercise workout. Verify correct haptic patterns. Test early advance override. Test completing final exercise transitions to completed state.

tasks.title: Workout Session History
tasks.description: Build a history view showing all past workout sessions with details, duration, and completion status.
tasks.details:
1. Create HistoryView as a tab:
   - List of past sessions sorted by date (newest first)
   - Each row: template name, date, duration, completion badge
   - Section headers by month
2. Create HistoryDetailView:
   - Session summary: template name, date, total duration
   - Exercise breakdown: each exercise with all set records
   - Display per-set: weight x reps
   - Completion percentage
3. Create HistoryViewModel:
   - Fetch sessions from SessionRepository
   - Support date range filtering
   - Calculate statistics
4. Swipe to delete sessions.
5. Empty state for no history yet.
6. Display streak (consecutive days with workouts).
tasks.testStrategy: Test history loads saved sessions correctly. Verify detail view shows all set records. Test date filtering. Test deletion removes session and associated records.

tasks.title: Tab Navigation & App Structure
tasks.description: Set up the main tab bar navigation with Today, Templates, History tabs and proper navigation flow.
tasks.details:
1. Create MainTabView with TabView:
   - Today (house icon) → TodayView
   - Templates (list icon) → TemplateListView
   - History (clock icon) → HistoryView
2. Configure NavigationStack for each tab.
3. Handle deep navigation: template editing, session detail, active workout.
4. Active workout should be presented as full-screen cover (not in tab).
5. Tab bar hidden during active workout.
6. Dark mode styling for tab bar.
7. Set Today as default/initial tab.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify all tabs navigate correctly. Test that active workout covers full screen. Verify tab bar hides during workout. Test back navigation in each tab.

tasks.title: Dark Mode Design System & UI Polish
tasks.description: Implement the app-wide design system with dark mode default, high contrast, large fonts, and consistent styling.
tasks.details:
1. Create a Design System:
   - Colors: Define color assets - background (#000000), surface (#1a1a1a), primary (#1a3a5c), accent, text, textSecondary
   - Typography: Define text styles - title (24pt), body (18pt), timer (72pt), timerStand (140pt), caption
   - Spacing: consistent padding/margin scale
2. Create reusable components:
   - GymBroButton: large CTA style (70pt height, full width, rounded corners)
   - GymBroCard: dark surface card with rounded corners
   - TimerDisplay: large monospaced number display
   - StepperControl: +/- with large touch targets (44x44pt minimum)
3. Force dark mode: .preferredColorScheme(.dark) on root view.
4. Apply design system to all existing views.
5. Ensure all interactive elements meet 44x44pt minimum touch target.
6. Support Dynamic Type for accessibility.
tasks.testStrategy: Visual review on multiple device sizes. Verify all touch targets meet 44x44pt minimum. Test Dynamic Type scaling. Verify dark mode is enforced.

tasks.title: Stand Mode (Hands-Free Display)
tasks.description: Implement Stand mode with extra-large centered text, minimal UI, and large bottom buttons optimized for viewing from a distance.
tasks.details:
1. Create StandModeView as an alternative layout for ActiveWorkoutView:
   - Timer: 140pt font, centered
   - Exercise name: large, centered below timer
   - Set progress: 'Serie [x]/[y]'
   - Weight display: large
   - Bottom buttons: PAUSA and SALTA (extra large, full width)
2. Toggle button to switch between normal and stand mode in ActiveWorkoutView.
3. Stand mode optimizations:
   - Minimal animations
   - High contrast (pure white on pure black)
   - No small text elements
   - Reduced refresh rate considerations
4. Auto-detect stand mode via device orientation or user preference.
5. Keep screen always on during stand mode.
6. Pulsanti grandi: minimum 60pt height in stand mode.
7. Transition animation between normal and stand mode.
tasks.testStrategy: Test readability from 1-2 meter distance. Verify all controls are usable with sweaty hands. Test mode switching during active workout. Verify timer accuracy in stand mode.

tasks.title: Progress Charts & Personal Records
tasks.description: Build progress visualization with weight-over-time charts, personal records display, and workout streak tracking.
tasks.details:
1. Create ProgressView as a tab or section:
   - Exercise weight chart (line chart over time, using Swift Charts)
   - Exercise selector to pick which exercise to view
   - Personal records section: max weight per exercise
   - Workout streak: consecutive days count with calendar heatmap
2. Create ProgressViewModel:
   - Query set records grouped by exercise
   - Calculate PR (personal record) per exercise
   - Calculate streak from session dates
   - Support time range: 1 week, 1 month, 3 months, all time
3. Charts implementation:
   - Use Swift Charts framework (iOS 16+)
   - Line chart: X = date, Y = max weight for that session
   - Bar chart option for volume (total sets * reps * weight)
4. PR display: highlight when new PR is achieved during workout.
5. Streak display: number + flame icon, reset if day missed.
6. Empty state for exercises without enough data.
tasks.testStrategy: Test charts render with varying data amounts. Verify PR detection is accurate. Test streak calculation with consecutive and non-consecutive dates. Test time range filtering.

tasks.title: HealthKit Integration
tasks.description: Integrate with HealthKit to write workout sessions as strength training workouts and read previous workout data.
tasks.details:
1. Create HealthKitService:
   - Request authorization for HKWorkoutType and HKQuantityType (activeEnergyBurned)
   - Write method: saveWorkout(session:setRecords:)
   - Read method: fetchRecentWorkouts(limit:) for showing external workouts
2. Writing HKWorkout:
   - activityType: .traditionalStrengthTraining
   - start/end dates from session
   - totalEnergyBurned: estimate from duration and intensity
   - metadata: include exercise names, total sets, total weight lifted
3. Prompt for HealthKit permission on first workout completion (opt-in).
4. Settings toggle to enable/disable HealthKit sync.
5. Show HealthKit status in settings (connected/not connected).
6. Handle authorization denied gracefully.
7. Add HealthKit capability in Xcode project and Info.plist usage descriptions.
tasks.testStrategy: Test on real device (HealthKit not available in simulator). Verify workout appears in Apple Health app. Test authorization flow (grant and deny). Test with HealthKit disabled.

tasks.title: Voice Commands (Speech Framework)
tasks.description: Implement local voice control using Speech Framework for hands-free workout control with commands: pausa, riprendi, avanti, indietro, ripeti.
tasks.details:
1. Create VoiceCommandService using Speech Framework:
   - SFSpeechRecognizer with locale: it-IT (Italian)
   - Continuous recognition during active workout
   - Local processing (on-device, no network required)
2. Supported commands mapping:
   - 'pausa' / 'stop' → pause timer
   - 'riprendi' / 'vai' → resume timer
   - 'avanti' / 'prossimo' → skip to next set/exercise
   - 'indietro' → go back to previous
   - 'ripeti' → repeat current set
3. Request permissions:
   - Microphone (NSMicrophoneUsageDescription)
   - Speech recognition (NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription)
4. Visual feedback: microphone icon indicating listening state.
5. Voice commands toggle in settings (off by default).
6. Fallback: all voice commands have corresponding physical buttons.
7. Handle noisy gym environment: require keyword prefix or activation gesture.
8. Stop recognition when workout ends or app backgrounds.
tasks.testStrategy: Test each voice command triggers correct action. Test in noisy environment simulation. Test with permissions denied. Verify fallback buttons always work. Test recognition stops on workout end.

tasks.title: Local Notifications for Background Timer
tasks.description: Implement local notifications to alert users when rest timer completes while app is backgrounded.
tasks.details:
1. Create NotificationService using UNUserNotificationCenter:
   - Request notification permission at first workout start
   - Schedule notification when entering REST_RUNNING state
   - Cancel notification when returning to foreground
2. Notification content:
   - Title: 'Tempo di recupero terminato'
   - Body: 'Prossimo: [next exercise/set name]'
   - Sound: default
3. Schedule with exact time interval matching remaining rest seconds.
4. Handle app returning to foreground: sync timer with elapsed time.
5. Cancel all pending notifications on workout completion or pause.
6. Update notification if user manually advances during rest.
7. Handle notification permission denied gracefully.
tasks.testStrategy: Test notification fires at correct time when app backgrounded. Test notification cancels on foreground return. Test timer syncs correctly after background period.

tasks.title: Settings Screen
tasks.description: Build a settings view for app configuration including HealthKit toggle, voice commands toggle, timer defaults, and data management.
tasks.details:
1. Create SettingsView with sections:
   - Workout Defaults: default rest time, weight step increment
   - Integrations: HealthKit toggle, Voice commands toggle
   - Data: Export CSV, Delete all data (with confirmation)
   - About: version, credits
2. Store settings in UserDefaults via @AppStorage.
3. HealthKit section: show connection status, link to Health app.
4. Voice commands section: show permission status.
5. Default rest time: stepper 15-300 seconds.
6. Weight step: picker for 1.0, 1.25, 2.0, 2.5, 5.0 kg.
7. Delete all data: require double confirmation.
8. Add Settings tab or gear icon in navigation.
tasks.testStrategy: Test all settings persist across app restarts. Test delete all data removes everything. Verify toggles correctly enable/disable features.

tasks.title: CSV Import/Export
tasks.description: Implement CSV export of workout history and import of workout templates for data portability.
tasks.details:
1. Export functionality:
   - Export all sessions as CSV: date, template, exercise, set, weight, reps, duration
   - Use ShareLink or UIActivityViewController
   - File name: GymBro_export_[date].csv
2. Import functionality:
   - Import CSV to create workout templates
   - Expected format: exercise name, sets, reps, rest
   - Use fileImporter modifier
   - Validate CSV structure before import
3. Export templates as shareable format.
4. Handle encoding (UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility).
5. Progress indicator for large exports.
tasks.testStrategy: Test export generates valid CSV. Test import creates correct templates. Test with malformed CSV. Test large dataset export performance.

tasks.title: iCloud Backup (Optional)
tasks.description: Implement optional iCloud backup for Core Data using NSPersistentCloudKitContainer for cross-device sync.
tasks.details:
1. Convert PersistenceController to support NSPersistentCloudKitContainer.
2. Make iCloud sync opt-in via settings toggle.
3. When disabled, use standard NSPersistentContainer.
4. Configure CloudKit container and entitlements.
5. Handle merge conflicts with last-write-wins strategy.
6. Show sync status indicator in settings.
7. Handle offline gracefully (works offline, syncs when connected).
8. Test data migration from local to cloud container.
tasks.testStrategy: Test with iCloud enabled and disabled. Test data syncs between two devices. Test offline usage still works. Test toggling sync on/off preserves data.

tasks.title: Accessibility & One-Handed UX Polish
tasks.description: Final accessibility pass ensuring VoiceOver support, Dynamic Type, minimum touch targets, and one-handed usability throughout the app.
tasks.details:
1. VoiceOver audit:
   - Add accessibilityLabel to all interactive elements
   - Add accessibilityHint for non-obvious actions
   - Ensure logical reading order
   - Timer announces remaining time periodically
2. Dynamic Type:
   - All text uses relative sizing
   - Test with largest accessibility size
   - Ensure layouts don't break
3. Touch targets:
   - Audit all buttons: minimum 44x44pt
   - Increase spacing between adjacent targets
   - Test with 'sweaty fingers' scenario (larger hit areas)
4. One-handed use:
   - All primary CTAs in bottom third of screen
   - No critical actions requiring top-of-screen reach
   - Swipe gestures as alternatives to buttons
5. Color contrast: verify WCAG AA compliance.
6. Reduce motion support: honor system accessibility settings.
tasks.testStrategy: Full VoiceOver walkthrough of every screen. Test with largest Dynamic Type. Measure all touch targets. Test one-handed use on largest iPhone. Run Accessibility Inspector audit.
